items.title: ChatLoopBackOff Episode 56 (​Kubescape)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>A CNCF sandbox project, Kubescape was created by ARMO as an open-source Kubernetes security platform to provides comprehensive security coverage. It offers hardening, posture management, and runtime security capabilities to ensure robust protection for Kubernetes environments.

Join CNCF Ambassador Shivay Lamba as he explores Kubescape for the first time.</article>

items.title: ChatLoopBackOff Episode 55 (​ChaosBlade)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>ChaosBlade is a cloud-native chaos engineering platform that supports multiple environments, clusters, and languages. With automated deployment experimental tools and support for experimental tool hosting, users can perform chaos experiments on the same interface.

Join CNCF Ambassador Nina Polshakova as she jumps into exploring Chaosblade for the first time.</article>

items.title: ChatLoopBackOff Episode 54 (​Flatcar)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Flatcar is a container-optimized operating system designed for running containers at scale. It provides Kubernetes clusters a stable, robust, and extensible foundation to run on top of. Its goals are to be high security and low maintenance with atomic updates and an immutable filesystem. A great choice in any cloud native stack.

Join CNCF Ambassador Seema Saharan as she explores Flatcar with you for the first time on ChatLoopBackOff!</article>

items.title: The State of GenAI & ML in the Cloud Native Ecosystem - Alejandro Saucedo & Bartosz Ocytko, Zalando
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Hong Kong, China (June 10-11); Tokyo, Japan (June 16-17); Hyderabad, India (August 6-7); Atlanta, US (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

The State of GenAI & ML in the Cloud Native Ecosystem - Alejandro Saucedo & Bartosz Ocytko, Zalando SE

The growth of GenAI & ML has brought new emerging challenges; in this talk we dive into the state of production of GenAI & ML in the cloud native ecosystem, where we provide an overview of trends, challenges, opportunities and tooling that the ecosystem is standardizing towards.

As part of this session, we will provide a snapshot of the current state of the ecosystem, as uncovered by recent surveys, which highlights the gaps in tooling and skills [1]. We will then cover the best practices and tooling that are arising from production use-cases of LLMOps/MLOps at scale to tackle domain-specific challenges such as agentic-workflows, AI guardrails, efficiency requirements - between others. These include the OSS frameworks [2] that are supporting the end-to-end LLMOps / MLOps lifecycle across pipelining, optimization, productionisation, monitoring/observability and ML safety.

[1] https://ethical.institute/state-of-ml...
[2] https://bit.ly/ml-list</article>
